title: heliotropium aucheri subsp. carmanicum
intro: Heliotropium aucheri subsp. carmanicum, also known as the Persian heliotrope, is a plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Heliotropium aucheri subsp. carmanicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its preparations include infusion, decoction, and tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
